zh.of, Treffynnon, Flintshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯福林爾霍利維爾
zh.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû"是Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû"和Angharad verch Ednyfed的兒子，於1400年出生在英國威爾斯福林爾霍利維爾。他和Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor至少有2個兒子。他去世於1447年在英國威爾斯享年47歲。

zh-hans.of, Treffynnon, Flintshire, Wales: 英国威尔斯福林爾霍利維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû"是Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû"和Angharad verch Ednyfed的儿子，于1400年出生在英国威尔斯福林爾霍利維爾。他和Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor至少有2个儿子。他去世于1447年在英国威尔斯享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ieuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: Iefan
meanings: Later form of   Ieuan .

variants.name: Ifan
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: "Fychan"
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Adda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of several old German names beginning with Ed-. The Elder and Younger Eddas are also the names of two influential Old Icelandic books of Norse myths and poems.

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

nameMeanings.name: Iorwerth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from iōr‘ ‘lord’ + a mutated form of berth ‘handsome’; borne in the Mabinogi by the jealous brother of Madawg , son of Maredudd . Iorwerth came to be regarded as a Welsh equivalent of Edward , but it has no actual connection with that name.

variants.name: Yorath
meanings: Anglicized form of   Iorwerth   or, in some cases, a transferred use of the surname derived from it.

nameMeanings.name: "Ddû"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: West African (Nigeria): from the Yoruba personal name Adú, originally a nickname from a dú ‘that has a very dark complexion’.
West African (Ghana): from an Akan male personal name of unexplained etymology.

en.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû" was born in 1400, in Holywell, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû" and Angharad verch Ednyfed. He had at least 2 sons with Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. He died in 1447, in Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 47.

fr.of, Treffynnon, Flintshire, Wales: Holywell, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû" est né en 1400, à Holywell, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, fils de Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû" et Angharad verch Ednyfed. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. Il est décédé en 1447, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû" wurde 1400 in Holywell, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû" und Angharad verch Ednyfed geboren. Er hatte mit Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1447 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Wales, United Kingdom.

Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. Morì nel 1447, nel Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû"は1400年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアホーリウエルでIeuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû"とAngharad verch Ednyfedの息子として生まれた。彼はAngharad Verch Howel ap Tudorとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1447年にイギリスウェールズで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû"(은)는 1400년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르 홀이웰에서 Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû"(와)과 Angharad verch Ednyfed의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1447년 영국 웨일스에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. Ele faleceu em 1447, em Gales, United Kingdom, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû" родился в 1400 г. в местности холивелл, флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû" и Angharad verch Ednyfed. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. Он умер в 1447 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Ieuan "Fychan" ab Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth  "Ddû" nació en 1400, en Holywell, Flintshire, Gales, Reino Unido como hijo de Ieuan ab Adda ab Iorwerth "Ddû" y Angharad verch Ednyfed. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Angharad Verch Howel ap Tudor. Murió en 1447, en Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años.
